_notes.nx_build: No build target exists for specimen-accessioning-orders-ui (library). Gate skipped per established precedent (see UOW-001 evaluation). Confirmed via prior evaluation obs-2115.
_notes.cypress_component_tests: Independent gate run: 10 passing, 0 failing, 0 pending in 523ms. Command: npx nx component-test specimen-accessioning-orders-ui --browser=chrome --spec='**/unsaved-changes-dialog/**/*.cy.ts' --skip-nx-cache
_notes.test_harness_script_anomaly: check-test-harnesses.py exited 1 with expected_harness='unsaved-changes-dialog.test-harness.ts'. This is a script naming-convention bug: the script strips '.component.ts' and appends '.test-harness.ts', but the codebase convention (verified against paperwork-label-dialog) uses '*.component.test-harness.ts'. The actual harness file unsaved-changes-dialog.component.test-harness.ts EXISTS and was read — gate outcome overridden to pass based on direct filesystem verification.

dod_completion.details: All 6 DoD items met with direct code evidence. Every item verified by reading source files and re-running the Cypress gate independently.

Dialog renders with Save & Continue, Dis.evidence: HTML template (lines 28-47) contains three <p-button> elements in pTemplate='footer' with data-test-id unsaved-changes-save-button, unsaved-changes-discard-button, unsaved-changes-cancel-button and labels 'Save & Continue', 'Discard & Continue', 'Cancel'. Cypress layout tests (cy.ts lines 63-76) assert each button is visible and contains the correct label text. Independent gate run: 10/10 pass.

Dialog integrates with application desig.evidence: component.ts imports only DialogModule and ButtonModule from primeng — same as existing dialogs. ViewEncapsulation.None matches existing dialog pattern. SCSS file contains only a .unsaved-changes-dialog__footer flexbox rule; no design-system token overrides. Template uses pTemplate='header'/'footer' pattern matching SessionTimeoutWarningModalComponent.

Dialog exposes an async API that resolve.evidence: service.ts line 36-39: open() sets visible signal to true and returns actionSubject$.asObservable().pipe(take(1)). resolve(action) on line 45-48 sets visible false and calls actionSubject$.next(action). Single-emission Observable — no memory leak. UOW-004 downstream contract documented in impl_report.

Cypress component test written covering.evidence: cy.ts lines 89-113: three tests in 'actions' describe block. Each calls harness.clickSave/Discard/Cancel() and asserts cy.get('@resolve').should('have.been.calledOnceWith', 'save'/'discard'/'cancel'). Independent gate confirms 10/10 pass including all 3 action tests.

Test harness created/updated with data-t.evidence: unsaved-changes-dialog.component.test-harness.ts lines 11-16 define saveButton(), discardButton(), cancelButton() selectors via data-test-id. Lines 35-37 expose clickSave(), clickDiscard(), clickCancel() actions. Harness exported from testing.ts barrel (line 3). UnsavedChangesDialogTestHarness type exported (line 40-42).

nx component-test passes with no failure.evidence: Independent evaluator gate run: 10 passing, 0 failing, 0 pending, 0 skipped in 523ms. NX reports: 'Successfully ran target component-test for project specimen-accessioning-orders-ui'. All specs passed.

scope_control.details: All 8 files modified are within UOW-003 scope. Six new files in the unsaved-changes-dialog directory, plus additive-only changes to src/index.ts (2 export lines) and src/testing.ts (1 export line). No unrelated refactors, no formatting changes to untouched code, no dependency updates.

breaking_change_risk.details: All changes are purely additive. UnsavedChangesDialogComponent and UnsavedChangesDialogService are new symbols — no existing contracts altered. Barrel additions to index.ts and testing.ts are append-only. No existing component signatures, route registrations, or data structures modified.

code_quality.details: Service and component follow established codebase patterns precisely. Signal + Subject + take(1) is idiomatic for single-emission async dialog decisions. OnPush + ViewEncapsulation.None matches existing dialogs. Code comments include WI-5035632/UOW-003 citations per standards. ESLint-disable comment is minimal, targeted, and necessary. No hacky shortcuts detected.

documentation_first.details: librarian_queries section present with two queries. Codebase checked for existing unsaved-changes dialog before creating new component (none found). Existing PrimeNG patterns (SessionTimeoutWarningModalComponent) used as implementation model rather than inventing new patterns. PrimeNG ConfirmationService correctly ruled out due to 2-action limitation; custom PrimeNG p-dialog component is the appropriate library-compliant choice.

notes: Clean first-attempt pass. The check-test-harnesses.py script has a naming-convention mismatch (expects '*.test-harness.ts' but codebase uses '*.component.test-harness.ts'). This is a tool bug — not an implementation defect. The harness file was directly verified to exist and contains correct selectors. Score docked 2 points for the uow_spec.yaml not being pre-written to the execution directory (minor pipeline hygiene gap, not the SE's responsibility for this stage).

metacognitive_context.decision_rationale: Evaluated gates sequentially then rubric in parallel with file reads. Test harness script anomaly detected by cross-referencing paperwork-label-dialog naming convention in same dialogs directory. Override of script result to 'pass' justified by direct filesystem evidence (file exists, read, contains correct selectors).

alternatives_discarded.approach: Accept check-test-harnesses.py exit code 1 as a real failure
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: Direct filesystem check confirms the harness file exists. Script uses wrong expected filename ('unsaved-changes-dialog.test-harness.ts') vs. actual codebase convention ('*.component.test-harness.ts'). Failing a clean implementation due to a script bug would be a false negative.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Downgrade overall_result to fail due to script anomaly
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: The spirit of the gate is to verify harness existence — that condition is met. All other gates and DoD items pass with direct evidence.

knowledge_gaps: Whether the check-test-harnesses.py script has been previously reported as having this naming-convention mismatch.

tool_anomalies.anomaly: Script expects harness at '*.test-harness.ts' (strips '.component.ts', appends '.test-harness.ts') but the mcs-products-mono-ui codebase uses '*.component.test-harness.ts' convention — evidenced by paperwork-label-dialog.component.test-harness.ts in the same dialogs directory. Script should use '.component.test-harness.ts' suffix or glob for any matching test-harness file in the same directory.
